examples.title: IPv4 Header Checksum Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate IPv4 header checksum using one's complement arithmetic
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateHeaderChecksum(Header[]):
 checksum = 0
 FOR i FROM 0 TO Length(Header)-1 STEP 2:
 // Combine two 8-bit bytes into one 16-bit word
 word = (Header[i] << 8) OR Header[i+1]
 // Add with carry wrap-around
 sum = checksum + word
 checksum = (sum AND 0xFFFF) + (sum >> 16)
 END FOR
 // One's complement of sum
 checksum = NOT checksum
 RETURN checksum
END FUNCTION

// Verification at destination:
FUNCTION VerifyHeaderChecksum(Header[]):
 checksum = 0
 FOR i FROM 0 TO Length(Header)-1 STEP 2:
 word = (Header[i] << 8) OR Header[i+1]
 sum = checksum + word
 checksum = (sum AND 0xFFFF) + (sum >> 16)
 END FOR
 result = checksum AND 0xFFFF
 RETURN (result == 0xFFFF) // All ones means valid
examples.explanation: Header checksum uses 16-bit one's complement arithmetic. All 16-bit words are summed with carry wrapping, then inverted. At each router, TTL changes so checksum must be recalculated.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = header length in 16-bit words

examples.title: IPv4 Fragmentation Algorithm
examples.description: Process of fragmenting a large IP datagram when MTU is smaller
examples.code:
FUNCTION FragmentDatagram(datagram, outgoingMTU):
 maxDataPerFragment = outgoingMTU - IP_HEADER_SIZE (20)
 originalData = datagram.data
 identification = GenerateUniqueID()
 offset = 0
 fragments = []
 
 WHILE offset < Length(originalData):
 // Determine data size for this fragment
 currentDataSize = min(maxDataPerFragment, Length(originalData) - offset)
 
 // Round down to 8-byte boundary except for last fragment
 IF (offset + currentDataSize) < Length(originalData):
 currentDataSize = currentDataSize - (currentDataSize MOD 8)
 END IF
 
 // Create fragment header
 fragment = New Datagram()
 fragment.header = CopyHeader(datagram.header)
 fragment.header.TotalLength = IP_HEADER_SIZE + currentDataSize
 fragment.header.Identification = identification
 fragment.header.FragmentOffset = offset / 8
 fragment.header.HeaderChecksum = 0 // To be calculated
 
 // Set MF flag
 IF (offset + currentDataSize) >= Length(originalData):
 fragment.header.MF = 0 // Last fragment
 ELSE:
 fragment.header.MF = 1
 END IF
 
 fragment.data = Substring(originalData, offset, currentDataSize)
 fragment.header.HeaderChecksum = CalculateChecksum(fragment.header)
 
 AddFragment(fragments, fragment)
 offset = offset + currentDataSize
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN fragments
examples.explanation: Fragmentation splits data into chunks that fit within MTU. Each fragment gets a copy of the original header with modified Total Length, MF flag, Fragment Offset, and recalculated checksum. All fragments share the same Identification.

examples.title: IPv4 Reassembly Algorithm
examples.description: Process of reassembling fragmented datagrams at destination
examples.explanation: Reassembly collects all fragments of a datagram identified by the same Identification value. Fragments must be in order (by offset) and contiguous. If timeout occurs before all fragments arrive, the partial data is discarded.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) for sorting fragments

examples.title: IPv4 Header Field Extraction
examples.description: Pseudocode to parse and extract important fields from IPv4 header
examples.explanation: This parsing is essential for network analysis and packet sniffing. Understanding bit-level extraction helps in debugging network issues and understanding how packets are processed.
